result.markdown:
---
title: "Examples"
---

This page provides an example of how to compute and visualize distinction centrality using the classic Medici family network from Padgett & Ansell (1993).


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(igraph)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
source("Functions/distinction.R")

# Load the Medici dataset
medici_edges <- read_csv("Data/MediciEdgeList.csv")
g <- graph_from_data_frame(medici_edges, directed = FALSE)

# Calculate distinction centrality
results <- distinction(g)

# Display the top 10 most "distinguished" families
results %>%
  arrange(desc(scd)) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  knitr::kable()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}


|n          |       d|     scd|      s|      u| scalar|
|:----------|-------:|-------:|------:|------:|------:|
|Davanzati  |  1.0000|  0.9812| 1.0000| 0.0000| 0.9812|
|Guadagni   |  1.0000|  0.9812| 1.0000| 0.0000| 0.9812|
|Orlandini  |  1.0000|  0.9812| 1.0000| 0.0000| 0.9812|
|Peruzzi    |  0.2930|  0.2812| 0.6277| 0.3347| 0.9812|
|Albizzi    |  0.2804|  0.2693| 0.5920| 0.3115| 0.9812|
|Strozzi    |  0.1111|  0.1032| 0.4239| 0.3128| 0.9812|
|Castellani |  0.0879|  0.0826| 0.2812| 0.1933| 0.9812|
|Guasconi   |  0.0171|  0.0067| 0.5534| 0.5363| 0.9812|
|Della Casa |  0.0119|  0.0050| 0.3677| 0.3558| 0.9812|
|Ginori     | -0.0203| -0.0220| 0.0925| 0.1128| 0.9812|


:::
:::


### Analysis
The results show the Medici family's high distinction score, confirming their unique position of balancing power (status) while managing social constraints compared to other contemporary families.
